work: Nafa'is al-Bayan — Commentary on al-Fara'id al-Hisan

notes: Arabic technical labels such as basmalah, fasilah, ra's al-ayah, Shami, and Iraqi are retained where helpful.
Collective labels are translated but not yet normalized into machine codes in this file.
The title markup inherited from the source HTML has been removed as a mechanical artifact.

In the Ra'd material, the phrase 'before it' is preserved as a statement of sequence in recitation, not as a restrictive qualifier, because the commentator explicitly says it functions only as bayan al-waqi'.
The closing summary of disputed places is separated as its own completion segment for easier later extraction.

segments.source_ar:
جديد النور سوى الكوفي عد
وللدمشقي البصير يعتمد
segments.translation_en: Count jadid and al-nur for everyone but the Kufan; as for al-basir, its counting is adopted for the Dimashqi.

segments.source_ar: وأقول: المعنى: أن قوله تعالى: ﴿إِنَّا لَفِي خَلْقٍ جَدِيد﴾ وقوله تعالى: ﴿أَمْ هَلْ تَسْتَوِي الظُّلُمَاتُ وَالنُّورُ﴾ عدهما غير الكوفي وتركهما الكوفي، وقوله تعالى: ﴿هَلْ يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمَى وَالْبَصِيرُ﴾ يعتمد عده للدمشقي دون سائر علماء العدد.
segments.translation_en: The meaning is that His statement {inna lafi khalqin jadid} and His statement {am hal tastawi al-zulumatu wa al-nur} are both counted by everyone other than the Kufan, while the Kufan omits them both. As for His statement {hal yastawi al-a'ma wa al-basir}, its counting is adopted for the Dimashqi alone, apart from the rest of the verse-counting authorities.

segments.source_ar:
سوء الحساب عد شام أولا ... وقبله الباطل للحمصي انجلا
من كل باب عده البصري ... وأيضا الشامي والكوفي
segments.translation_en: Count the first su' al-hisab for the Shami; and before it, al-batil stands clear for the Himsi. Min kulli bab is counted by the Basran, and also by the Shami and the Kufan.

segments.source_ar: وأقول: قوله تعالى: ﴿أُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ سُوءُ الْحِسَاب﴾ وهو الموضع الأول عده الشامي وتركه غيره. وقيدته بالموضع الأول لإخراج الثاني المتفق على عده وهو ﴿وَيَخَافُونَ سُوءَ الْحِسَاب﴾ وقوله تعالى: ﴿كَذَلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللَّهُ الْحَقَّ وَالْبَاطِلَ﴾ معدود للحمصي وحده. وقولي: "وقبله" ليس قيدا للاحتراز إنما هو لبيان الواقع وهو أن ﴿كَذَلِكَ يَضْرِبُ اللَّهُ الْحَقَّ وَالْبَاطِلَ﴾ وقع في التلاوة قبل: ﴿أُولَئِكَ لَهُمْ سُوءُ الْحِسَاب﴾ وقوله تعالى: ﴿وَالْمَلائِكَةُ يَدْخُلُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ كُلِّ بَابٍ﴾ عده البصري والشامي والكوفي وتركه الحجازيون المدنيان والمكي.
segments.translation_en: His statement {ula'ika lahum su' al-hisab}, which is the first occurrence, is counted by the Shami and omitted by the others. I qualified it as the first occurrence in order to exclude the second, which is unanimously counted, namely {wa yakhafuna su'a al-hisab}. His statement {kathalika yadribu Allahu al-haqqa wa al-batil} is counted by the Himsi alone. My words 'before it' are not a restrictive qualifier; they serve only to describe the actual sequence, namely that {kathalika yadribu Allahu al-haqqa wa al-batil} occurs earlier in the recitation than {ula'ika lahum su' al-hisab}. And His statement {wa al-mala'ikatu yadkhuluna 'alayhim min kulli bab} is counted by the Basran, the Shami, and the Kufan, and omitted by the Hijazis—the two Madinans and the Makkan.

segments.source_ar: والخلاصة أن مواضع الخلاف في هذه السورة ستة: جديد، والنور، والبصير، سوء الحساب، والباطل، من كل باب، وتأمل من عد ومن ترك والله أعلم.
segments.translation_en: To sum up, the disputed places in this surah are six: jadid, al-nur, al-basir, su' al-hisab, al-batil, and min kulli bab. Reflect on who counts and who omits. God knows best.
